title: Jeffrey Niehaus
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Jeffrey Niehaus
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
published_college: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

organizational_affiliations.label: College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences

biography: I grew up in St. Louis, MO. I got my undergraduate degree in psychology from Purdue University in 1996, my Master's in Counseling from the University of Denver in 1999, and my PhD in Developmental and Evolutionary Psychology from the University of California at Santa Barbara in 2009. Since then I've worked as a Senior Instructor at the University of Victoria in British Columbia, Canada and here at CNU. My wife is Jayne, and I have a son (Kurt) and a daughter (Maria).
education_entries: Ph D in Psychology, University of California, Santa Barbara
MA in Counseling Psychology, University of Denver
research_interests: Game theory, theory of mind, and all topics evolutionary.
teaching_interests: Development, evolutionary, morality, philosophy of science, statistics, and etc.
awards_honors: Exceptional Organization Advisor, Student Leadership Awards. (2017)
